response: Charles A. Agemian and Arshile Gorky had different career paths. Charles A. Agemian was an American banker who retired from Chase Manhattan Bank and became chairman of Hackensack Trust Co., later renamed Garden State National Bank. He did not have a career in the arts. Arshile Gorky, on the other hand, was an Armenian-American painter who had a significant career in the art world, influenced by the Armenian Genocide and known for his contributions to Abstract Expressionism. Gorky's career was largely centered on his artistic endeavors, while Agemian's career was in banking. Therefore, based on the information provided, Arshile Gorky had more career changes in the artistic field compared to Charles A. Agemian.
